rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO** IN THE MATTER OF THE BERNARD LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/2020 to allow a court investigator's report on the related conservatorship to be completed. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The bond must be increased before this account can be approved. An ex parte petition to increase the bond must be filed. LRSF 14.40A. 2) Did the cash allowances to the beneficiary reported on Schedule C reduce or otherwise affect the beneficiary's entitlement to public benefits? 3) Has the 2016 assessment for the related conservatorship case been paid? 4) Schedule E refers to "Ameriprise", but the ending account statement shows "Ameritrade". 5) What is the number of Spectra fractional shares converted to cash of $27.39 as reported on Schedule G1? 6) Thomas Bernard's declaration (filed on 10/19/2020) does not include his printout verification required by CRC 7.575(b)(1). 7) The paralegal spent 1.1 hours on 02/19/2019 & 08/29/2019 on the petition for change of venue, but another paralegal appears to have worked on the same petition in July 2017. Around the same time, the attorney appears to have spent 0.9 hours on the same petition. 8) The paralegal spent more than 13 hours on this account between 02/13/2020 & 05/21/2020. Was the paralegal's time productively spent without duplicating the work delegated to the accountant? 9) Why was the SSA questioning the exempt status of this trust during this account period?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTHONY VIDAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/20. A First Amended Petition for First and Final Distribution and Accounting, etc., was filed on 10/27/20, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. No notice of the fully amended petition has been given (or waived), including to the party requesting special notice. Original bank statements must be filed to verify the cash on hand. [LRSF 14.62.] A declaration must be filed re the retention of the original statements. [LRSF 2.11 M.4.] A copy of the seller's closing statement must be filed to verify the costs of sale. The 2951 shares of Aegon were appraised at $24,268.73, not $24,286.73. Probate accountings are cash, not accrual, accountings; unrealized gains or losses are not gains or losses on sale, which are the gains or losses that must be reflected in an accounting. Refunds for amounts paid during estate administration are not receipts includable in the fee base. The incorrect order in the predeceased brother's estate cannot be amended in this estate. If notice to the FTB was just mailed on 9/21/20, the FTB has 60 days to file a claim, so the estate cannot close until after 11/21/20 in any event. A petition for final distribution must set forth the exact assets to be distributed, including the net amount of cash. [LRSF 14.67 L.7.] An amount in excess of the FDIC insured amount is all being kept in one account that is reportedly also "shared" with the trust. How can title to an account be held jointly between the personal representative of an estate and the trustee of a trust? (The time for filing claims expired four months after Letters were issued, or 6/28/18, not 10/30/17. The I&As filed to correct the "ministerial" errors were filed on 10/26/20, not 9/17/20.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YVES DENIS MOUISSET
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/20. A petition for final distribution must set forth the exact assets to be distributed, including the net amount of cash as of a date certain after payment of fees and costs requested; "balance of Property on Hand" is not sufficient. Paragraph 32 of the petition is incomplete. Was notice given to the FTB and, if so, when? Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary was previously confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. One of the attorney costs of $25.50 appears to be a duplicate. Why was 1-800-Got-Junk paid $2,130.00 and why were groceries and a car rental necessary? (The administrator was granted full, not limited, IAEA authority.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SAN FRANCISCO SPCA HEARING DOG PROGRAM TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Modify Charitable Purpose Of Institutional Fund
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 7th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may include): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) The petition requests the charitable purpose of the fund in support of the San Francisco SPCA's former Hearing Dog program is modified to permit use of the Fund to support specific SF-SPCA programs which focus on the human-animal bond including the Life of Riley Fund, the Animal Assisted Therapy program, and programs which provide charitable veterinary care to assist indigent persons with pets.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANDREW R. DOEDEN MINOR'S SETTLEMENT TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Governement Bonds, And Approving Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Hearing issues have been e-mailed to counsel. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CANTILLANO IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Irrevocable Trust, To Invalidate Trust Amendment, For Return Of Trust Property, For Intentional Interference
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/20. (No earlier availability.) The incorrect form of notice was served. Additionally, if other heirs have an interest in the property, they must be personally served notice. [Probate Code Section 851(a).] The prayer requests that all trust property be returned, but does not clearly identify the property nor to whom it should be returned. (if the trust is determined to be invalid, to whom or what entity would the property be returned?)
